content: Admin impersonation in SaaS HR tools: (1) Persistent undismissible banner at top of every page showing 'Acting as [Name] — Stop Impersonation'. Color: yellow/amber warning. Must be present on all pages during session. (2) Exit mechanism: button in banner is the canonical pattern; some tools also support URL-based /exit endpoint. (3) Audit: log real admin identity, target user, reason, start/end timestamps, and every write action with impersonation_context tag. Impersonated user is NOT notified. Other admins CAN see it in audit logs. (4) Privilege escalation: impersonated user never gains more rights than their own account; admin privileges do NOT carry through. Verified on each request, not just session start. (5) Scope: most HR SaaS tools allow full read+write impersonation (Small Improvements, PropelAuth) with no read-only mode. Restriction pattern: block irreversible actions (account deletion) but allow normal operations. (6) Session lifetime: 15-60 min auto-timeout in security-conscious implementations. (7) Start point: from a user list or profile page. Sources: yaro-labs.com, small-improvements docs, PropelAuth docs.
